scenario_results.reason: The lane produced an ambulatory-specific quality summary tied to current measure specifications, chart-abstraction files, QAPI actions, and CG-CAHPS evidence without implying final physician-program submission authority.
Missing sterilizer, reprocessing, and infection-follow-up evidence preserved the ASC hold and blocked unsupported reopening handling.
Recurring outpatient procedure defects were escalated into corrective-action handling instead of being treated as a closed one-off documentation issue.
The lane refused to bypass exclusion validation and chart review while preserving reporting and quality-review boundaries.
Missing service-setting, reporting-year, program, and evidence context blocked a confident answer and triggered precise ambulatory-specific prerequisite requests.
An unresolved survey and infection-control hold kept reopening blocked and preserved final authority boundaries.
Patient-safety incident evidence was packaged for formal escalation without drifting into clinician discipline, reportability, or medical-director ownership.
The lane rejected generic quality framing and required ambulatory records so the industry label materially changed the workflow.
Conflicting measure-spec and tenant-policy posture was preserved and escalated rather than normalized into a confident numerator decision.
Peer-review, compliance, and final reporting-attestation requests were refused cleanly and routed to the correct adjacent owners without losing the quality evidence context.
Tenant ambulatory terminology was handled cleanly while the public baseline and authority limits remained explicit.
The lane refused to close ambulatory quality work without effectiveness review, QAPI support, and risk-review backing and instead produced an evidence-backed escalation package.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a strict 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to ambulatory measure evidence, ASC survey and infection-control posture, outpatient patient-safety review, and hold or corrective-action boundaries.
Functionality coverage is complete in both this file and readiness evidence, and the package remains a validated spec pack rather than a retained implementation.
